channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: Seiko USA website defaced as hacker claims customer data theft
item.description: The Seiko USA website was defaced over the weekend, displaying a message from attackers claiming they stole its Shopify customer database and threatening to leak it unless a ransom is paid. [...]

item.title: Context.ai OAuth Token Compromise
item.description: Compromised Context.ai OAuth tokens enabled attackers to perform a supply chain attack via trusted SaaS integrations. Learn how to assess the risk in your environment and how to prevent the next attack.

item.title: Scattered Spider member Tyler Buchanan pleads guilty to major crypto theft
item.description: Tyler Buchanan, linked to Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty in the US to hacking companies and stealing millions in cryptocurrency. Tyler Buchanan, a 24-year-old from Scotland linked to the Scattered Spider group, admi...

item.title: SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8) Enables RCE via Malicious GGUF Model Files
item.description: A critical security vulnerability has been disclosed in SGLang that, if successfully exploited, could result in remote code execution on susceptible systems. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-5760, carries a CVSS...

item.title: Vulnerability Summary for the Week of April 13, 2026
item.description: High Vulnerabilities Primary Vendor -- Product Description Published CVSS Score Source Info Patch Info Grafana--Pyroscope Pyroscope is an open-source continuous profiling database. The database supports various storag...

item.title: ​​Supply Chain Compromise Impacts Axios Node Package Manager​
item.description: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) is releasing this alert to provide guidance in response to the software supply chain compromise of the Axios node package manager (npm). 1 Axios is an HTTP c...

item.title: Frontier AI Is Collapsing the Exploit Window. Here’s How Defenders Must Respond.
ZionSiphon Malware Targets Water Infrastructure Systems
item.description: ZionSiphon malware targets OT water systems with sabotage and ICS scanning capabilities

item.title: Serial-to-IP Converter Flaws Expose OT and Healthcare Systems to Hacking
item.description: Forescout researchers discovered 20 new vulnerabilities in Lantronix and Silex products and described theoretical attack scenarios. The post Serial-to-IP Converter Flaws Expose OT and Healthcare Systems to Hacking app...

item.title: How to clone an AWS CloudHSM cluster across Regions
item.description: Important: As of January 1, 2025, Client SDK 3 tools (CMU and KMU) are no longer supported. This guide has been updated to use Client SDK 5 commands exclusively. Ensure you’re using the latest Client SDK 5 version (5....

item.title: Microsoft: Teams increasingly abused in helpdesk impersonation attacks
item.description: Microsoft is warning of threat actors increasingly abusing external Microsoft Teams collaboration and relying on legitimate tools for access and lateral movement on enterprise networks. [...]

item.title: Formbook Malware Campaign Uses Multiple Obfuscation Techniques to Avoid Detection
item.description: Formbook attacks use combination of DLL Side-Loading and Obfuscated JavaScript to stay hidden, researchers at WatchGuard have uncovered

item.title: Vercel breached via compromised third-party AI tool
item.description: Cloud deployment and hosting platform Vercel has suffered a security breach that resulted in attackers accessing some of its internal systems and compromising Vercel credentials of a “limited subset of customers”. Adv...

item.title: The backup myth that is putting businesses at risk
item.description: Backups protect data, but don't keep your business running during downtime. Datto shows why BCDR is essential to keep operations running during ransomware and outages. [...]

item.title: CVE-2023-33538 under attack for a year, but exploitation still unsuccessful
item.description: Hackers have targeted CVE-2023-33538 flaw in old TP-Link routers for a year, but no successful exploitation has been seen so far. Hackers have been trying for over a year to exploit a serious flaw, tracked as CVE-2023...

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Vercel Hack, Push Fraud, QEMU Abused, New Android RATs Emerge & More
item.description: Monday’s recap shows the same pattern in different places. A third-party tool becomes a way in, then leads to internal access. A trusted download path is briefly swapped to deliver malware. Browser extensions act norm...

item.title: British Scattered Spider hacker pleads guilty to crypto theft charges
item.description: A British man, believed to be the leader of the Scattered Spider cybercrime collective, has pleaded guilty in the United States to charges of wire fraud and aggravated identity theft. [...]

item.title: Building the agentic cloud: everything we launched during Agents Week 2026
item.description: Agents Week 2026 is a wrap. Let’s take a look at everything we announced, from compute and security to the agent toolbox, platform tools, and the emerging agentic web. Everything we shipped for the agentic cloud.

item.title: The AI engineering stack we built internally — on the platform we ship
item.description: We built our internal AI engineering stack on the same products we ship. That means 20 million requests routed through AI Gateway, 241 billion tokens processed, and inference running on Workers AI, serving more than 3...

item.title: Orchestrating AI Code Review at scale
item.description: Learn about how we built a CI-native AI code reviewer using OpenCode that helps our engineers ship better, safer code.

item.title: Frontier AI Is Collapsing the Exploit Window. Here’s How Defenders Must Respond.
Attackers Exploit DVR Command Injection Flaw to Deploy Mirai-Based Botnet
item.description: FortiGuard Labs has identified a Mirai-based Nexcorium campaign actively exploiting CVE-2024-3721 in TBK DVR devices

item.title: When AI Accelerates the Offense, Coverage Gaps Become Catastrophic
item.description: The window between a vulnerability existing and an attacker weaponizing it is collapsing. Anthropic’s new seven-part framework for AI-accelerated threats confirms what cloud security teams are already feeling and poin...

item.title: British Scattered Spider Hacker Pleads Guilty in the US
item.description: Tyler Buchanan admitted in court to hacking into various companies, defrauding them, and stealing cryptocurrency from multiple individuals. The post British Scattered Spider Hacker Pleads Guilty in the US appeared fir...

item.title: Wiz and Databricks: Adding Databricks to the Wiz Security Graph
item.description: Extending Wiz Visibility with the Databricks Data & AI Platform

item.title: From Code to Pipeline: Wiz Code Now Secures Your Build Environment
item.description: Threat actors have moved upstream, and while security teams have focused on the code developers write, the systems that build and deliver that code have remained a blind spot.

item.title: Hackers Abuse QEMU for Defense Evasion
item.description: The machine emulator has been abused in at least two different campaigns distributing ransomware and remote access tools. The post Hackers Abuse QEMU for Defense Evasion appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Why Most AI Deployments Stall After the Demo
item.description: The fastest way to fall in love with an AI tool is to watch the demo. Everything moves quickly. Prompts land cleanly. The system produces impressive outputs in seconds. It feels like the beginning of a new era for you...

item.title: AI platform ATHR makes voice phishing a one-person job
item.description: For $4,000 and a cut of the take, a lone criminal can now run a fully automated voice-phishing operation via ATHR, a plaform that spoofs emails alerts from Google, Microsoft, and Coinbase, buries a phone number in eac...

item.title: Is “Satoshi Nakamoto” Really Adam Back?
item.description: The New York Times has a long article where the author lays out an impressive array of circumstantial evidence that the inventor of Bitcoin is the cypherpunk Adam Back. I don’t know. The article is convincing, but it’...

item.title: Bluesky Disrupted by Sophisticated DDoS Attack
item.description: A pro-Iran hacker group has taken credit for the attack on Bluesky, which appears to have lasted 24 hours. The post Bluesky Disrupted by Sophisticated DDoS Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Anthropic MCP Design Vulnerability Enables RCE, Threatening AI Supply Chain
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a critical "by design" weakness in the Model Context Protocol's (MCP) architecture that could pave the way for remote code execution and have a cascading effect on the artific...

item.title: Third-party AI hack triggers Vercel breach, internal environments accessed
item.description: Vercel suffered a breach after a hacked Context.ai tool exposed an employee account, letting attackers access limited internal systems and non-sensitive data. Vercel reported a security breach caused by the compromise...

item.title: Senate Extends Surveillance Powers Until April 30 After Chaotic Votes in House
item.description: The Senate approved a short-term renewal until April 30 of a controversial surveillance program used by U.S. spy agencies. The post Senate Extends Surveillance Powers Until April 30 After Chaotic Votes in House appear...

item.title: Fracturing Software Security With Frontier AI Models
item.description: Unit 42 finds frontier AI models enhance vulnerability discovery, acting as full-spectrum security researchers. They enable autonomous zero-day discovery and faster N-day patching. The post Fracturing Software Securit...

item.title: Half of the 6 Million Internet-Facing FTP Servers Lack Encryption
item.description: The continued use of the half-century-old protocol exposes enterprises and end users to various types of attacks. The post Half of the 6 Million Internet-Facing FTP Servers Lack Encryption appeared first on SecurityWe...

item.title: NCSC Outlines Coordinated Plan to Boost NHS Cyber Resilience
item.description: The National Cyber Security Centre has shared an update of its resilience-building efforts for the NHS

item.title: Microsoft releases emergency updates to fix Windows Server issues
item.description: Microsoft has released out-of-band (OOB) updates to fix issues affecting Windows Server systems after installing the April 2026 security updates. [...]

item.title: Next.js Creator Vercel Hacked
item.description: Vercel confirmed suffering a breach after a hacker claiming to be part of ShinyHunters offered to sell stolen data for $2 million. The post Next.js Creator Vercel Hacked appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: AI Model Claude Opus turns bugs into exploits for just $2,283
item.description: Claude Opus created a working Chrome exploit for $2,283, showing that widely available AI models can already find and weaponize vulnerabilities. Claude Opus managed to produce a functional Chrome exploit for just $2,2...

item.title: Meta and PortSwigger drive offensive security further to find what others miss
item.description: Meta Bug Bounty and PortSwigger have formed a partnership to help security researchers sharpen their skills, collaborate more closely, and improve vulnerability discovery. The initiative combines Meta’s bug bounty pro...

item.title: Researchers Detect ZionSiphon Malware Targeting Israeli Water, Desalination OT Systems
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new malware called ZionSiphon that appears to be specifically designed to target Israeli water treatment and desalination systems. The malware has been codenamed ZionSiphon by...

item.title: Hackers Fail to Exploit Flaw in Discontinued TP-Link Routers
item.description: In-the-wild exploitation has been ongoing for a year, but no successful payload execution has been observed. The post Hackers Fail to Exploit Flaw in Discontinued TP-Link Routers appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Handling the CVE Flood With EPSS, (Mon, Apr 20th)
item.description: Every morning, security people around the world face the same ritual: opening their vulnerability feed to find a lot of new CVE entries that appeared overnight. Over the past decade, this flood has become a defining c...

item.title: EU pushes for stronger cloud sovereignty, awards €180 million to four providers
item.description: The European Commission is stepping up efforts to strengthen the EU’s digital sovereignty by awarding a cloud services tender worth up to €180 million over six years. The initiative gives EU institutions and agencies...

item.title: SmokedMeat: Open-source tool shows what attackers do inside CI/CD pipelines
item.description: Boost Security has released SmokedMeat, an open-source framework that runs attack chains against CI/CD infrastructure so engineering and security teams can see what an attacker would do in their specific environment....

item.title: How to spot a North Korean fake in a job interview
item.description: North Korean operatives are getting hired at companies by passing job interviews using fake identities and AI tools. In this Help Net Security video, Adrian Cheek, a senior cybercrime researcher at Flare, outlines sev...

item.title: Product showcase: Syncthing for secure, private file synchronization
item.description: Syncthing is a free and open-source application that synchronizes files directly between your devices. Instead of uploading data to a central server, it uses a peer-to-peer approach, transferring files whenever peers...

item.title: Vercel Breach Tied to Context AI Hack Exposes Limited Customer Credentials
item.description: Web infrastructure provider Vercel has disclosed a security breach that allows bad actors to gain unauthorized access to "certain" internal Vercel systems. The incident stemmed from the compromise of Context.ai, a thi...

item.title: ISC Stormcast For Monday, April 20th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9898, (Mon, Apr 20th)
Vercel confirms breach as hackers claim to be selling stolen data
item.description: Cloud development platform Vercel has disclosed a security incident after threat actors claimed to have breached its systems and are attempting to sell stolen data. [...]

item.title: Apple account change alerts abused to send phishing emails
item.description: Apple account change notifications are being abused to send fake iPhone purchase phishing scams within legitimate emails sent from Apple's servers, increasing legitimacy and potentially allowing them to bypass spam fi...

item.title: Cyber attacks fuel surge in cargo theft across logistics industry
item.description: Hackers infiltrate logistics firms to steal cargo and divert payments, cyberattacks are linked to organized crime and rising losses. Proofpoint researchers observed crooks targeting trucking and logistics companies, r...

item.title: NIST to stop rating non-priority flaws due to volume increase
item.description: The National Institute of Standards and Technology will stop assigning severity scores to lower-priority vulnerabilities due to the growing workload from rising submission volumes. [...]

item.title: Security Affairs newsletter Round 573 by Pierluigi Paganini – INTERNATIONAL EDITION
item.description: A new round of the weekly Security Affairs newsletter has arrived! Every week, the best security articles from Security Affairs are free in your email box. Enjoy a new round of the weekly SecurityAffairs newsletter, i...

item.title: SECURITY AFFAIRS MALWARE NEWSLETTER ROUND 93
item.description: Security Affairs Malware newsletter includes a collection of the best articles and research on malware in the international landscape CPU-Z / HWMonitor watering hole infection – a copy-pasted attack Fake Claude site i...

item.title: Week in review: Acrobat Reader flaw exploited, Claude Mythos offensive capabilities and limits
item.description: Here’s an overview of some of last week’s most interesting news, articles, interviews and videos: Bringing governance and visibility to machine and AI identities In this Help Net Security interview, Archit Lohokare, C...

item.title: Hidden VMs: how hackers leverage QEMU to stealthily steal data and spread malware
item.description: Attackers abuse QEMU to hide malware in virtual machines, bypass detection, steal data, and deploy ransomware without leaving any trace. Sophos researchers report a rise in attackers abusing QEMU, an open-source emula...

item.title: Tycoon 2FA Loses Phishing Kit Crown Amid Surge in Attacks
item.description: Threat actors are reusing Tycoon 2FA tools across other phishing kits following the platform’s disruption. The post Tycoon 2FA Loses Phishing Kit Crown Amid Surge in Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Nexcorium Mirai variant exploits TBK DVR flaw to launch DDoS attacks
item.description: A Mirai variant called Nexcorium exploits a flaw in TBK DVRs to infect devices and use them in DDoS attacks, along with outdated TP-Link routers. Fortinet researchers found that threat actors are exploiting vulnerabil...

item.title: [Webinar] Eliminate Ghost Identities Before They Expose Your Enterprise Data
item.description: In 2024, compromised service accounts and forgotten API keys were behind 68% of cloud breaches. Not phishing. Not weak passwords. Unmanaged non-human identities that nobody was watching. For every employee in your org...

item.title: Microsoft Defender under attack as three zero-days, two of them still unpatched, enable elevated access
item.description: Attackers exploit three Microsoft Defender zero-days, code-named BlueHammer, RedSun, and UnDefend, to gain elevated access. Attackers are exploiting three recently disclosed zero-day flaws in Microsoft Defender to gai...

item.title: Mirai Variant Nexcorium Exploits CVE-2024-3721 to Hijack TBK DVRs for DDoS Botnet
item.description: Threat actors are exploiting security flaws in TBK DVR and end‑of‑life (EoL) TP-Link Wi-Fi routers to deploy Mirai-botnet variants on compromised devices, according to findings from Fortinet FortiGuard Labs and Palo A...

item.title: Critical flaw in Protobuf library enables JavaScript code execution
item.description: Proof-of-concept exploit code has been published for a critical remote code execution flaw in protobuf.js, a widely used JavaScript implementation of Google's Protocol Buffers. [...]

item.title: NAKIVO v11.2: Ransomware Defense, Faster Replication, vSphere 9, and Proxmox VE 9.0 Support
item.description: NAKIVO Inc. announced the general availability of NAKIVO Backup & Replication v11.2, focused on fast, reliable, and proactive data protection. [...]

item.title: Threat Brief: Escalation of Cyber Risk Related to Iran (Updated April 17)
item.description: Unit 42 details recent Iranian cyberattack activity, sharing direct observations of phishing, hacktivist activity and cybercrime. We include recommendations for defenders. The post Threat Brief: Escalation of Cyber Ri...

item.title: Transform security logs into OCSF format using a configuration-driven ETL solution
item.description: Security logs capture essential security-related activities, such as user sign-ins, file access, network traffic, and application usage. These logs are important for monitoring, detecting, and responding to potential...

item.title: Coast Guard's New Cybersecurity Rules Offers Lessons for CISOs
item.description: The Maritime Transportation Security Act (MTSA) requires plans to protect OT systems, audits by independent third parties, and a hybrid OT-security role.

item.title: Webinar: From phishing to fallout — Why MSPs must rethink both security and recovery
item.description: Cyberattacks are evolving faster than many MSP and corporate defenses can keep up, with phishing driving much of today's cybercrime. Join our upcoming webinar to learn how to combine security and recovery strategies t...

item.title: In Other News: Satellite Cybersecurity Act, $90K Chrome Flaw, Teen Hacker Arrested
item.description: Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: ShinyHunters targets Rockstar Games, ShowDoc vulnerability exploited in the wild, and EPA to boost cybersecurity budget to $19 million. The post In Oth...

item.title: Mythos and Cybersecurity
item.description: Last week, Anthropic pulled back the curtain on Claude Mythos Preview , an AI model so capable at finding and exploiting software vulnerabilities that the company decided it was too dangerous to release to the public....

item.title: Google wipes out 602 million scam ads with Gemini on duty
item.description: Google claims that its security teams work around the clock using its Gemini AI models to detect and stop harmful ads. “Bad actors are using generative AI to create deceptive ads at scale, and Gemini helps us detect a...

item.title: Google Blocks 8.3B Policy-Violating Ads in 2025, Launches Android 17 Privacy Overhaul
item.description: Google this week announced a new set of Play policy updates to strengthen user privacy and protect businesses against fraud, even as it revealed it blocked or removed over 8.3 billion ads globally and suspended 24.9 m...

item.title: Another DraftKings Hacker Sentenced to Prison
item.description: Kamerin Stokes sold stolen credentials through an online marketplace even after pleading guilty to his role in the DraftKings attack. The post Another DraftKings Hacker Sentenced to Prison appeared first on SecurityWe...

item.title: Lawmakers Gathered Quietly to Talk About AI. Angst and Fears of ‘Destruction’ Followed
item.description: Thursday’s discussion comes as leaders on Capitol Hill grapple with the dizzying pace of global developments in which technology plays a central role. The post Lawmakers Gathered Quietly to Talk About AI. Angst and Fe...

item.title: Recently leaked Windows zero-days now exploited in attacks
item.description: Threat actors are exploiting three recently disclosed Windows security vulnerabilities in attacks aimed at gaining SYSTEM or elevated administrator permissions. [...]

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: New Giant Squid Video
item.description: Pretty fantastic video from Japan of a giant squid eating another squid. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t covered. Blog moderation policy.

item.title: Kyrgyzstan-based crypto exchange Grinex shuts down after $13.7M cyber heist, blames Western Intelligence
item.description: Grinex halted operations after a $13.7M hack, blaming Western intelligence. Stolen funds came from wallets of Russian users on the platform. Kyrgyz crypto exchange Grinex halted operations after a threat actor stole $...

item.title: How NIST's Cutback of CVE Handling Impacts Cyber Teams
item.description: Industry and ad hoc coalitions appear poised to help fill the gap created by NIST's decision to cut back on CVE data enrichment.

item.title: Payouts King ransomware uses QEMU VMs to bypass endpoint security
item.description: The Payouts King ransomware is using the QEMU emulator as a reverse SSH backdoor to run hidden virtual machines on compromised systems and bypass endpoint security. [...]

item.title: Tycoon 2FA Phishers Scatter, Adopt Device Code Phishing
item.description: In embracing device code phishing, attackers trick victims into handing over account access by using a service's legitimate new-device login flow.

item.title: White House Chief of Staff to Meet With Anthropic CEO Over Its New AI Technology
item.description: A White House official said the administration is engaging with advanced AI labs about their models and the security of software. The post White House Chief of Staff to Meet With Anthropic CEO Over Its New AI Technolo...

item.title: IaC Inventory: A Unified View Across Code, Deployments, and Cloud
item.description: As AI applications introduce a new class of infrastructure resources, visibility into what your IaC creates, where it runs, and whether it has drifted has never been more critical.

item.title: CoChat Launches AI Collaboration Platform to Combat Shadow AI
item.description: CoChat is fundamentally an AI collaboration platform designed for teamwork and to bring visibility and governance into enterprise AI shadows. The post CoChat Launches AI Collaboration Platform to Combat Shadow AI appe...

item.title: Every Old Vulnerability Is Now an AI Vulnerability
item.description: AI's danger isn't that it's creating new bugs, it's that it's amplifying old ones.

item.title: DraftKings hacker sentenced to prison, ordered to pay $1.4 Million
item.description: A DraftKings hacker got 30 months in prison for selling stolen credentials and must pay over $1.4 million in fines and restitution. Kamerin Stokes, 23, from Memphis (aka TheMFNPlug), received a 30-month prison sentenc...

item.title: Inside an Underground Guide: How Threat Actors Vet Stolen Credit Card Shops
item.description: In cybercrime markets, trust isn't assumed, it's verified. Flare reveals how underground guides teach actors to evaluate carding shops based on data quality, reputation, and survivability. [...]

item.title: Three Microsoft Defender Zero-Days Actively Exploited; Two Still Unpatched
item.description: Huntress is warning that threat actors are exploiting three recently disclosed security flaws in Microsoft Defender to gain elevated privileges in compromised systems. The activity involves the exploitation of three v...

item.title: Commercial AI Models Show Rapid Gains in Vulnerability Research
item.description: AI models are making rapid gains in vulnerability research and exploit development, raising new cybersecurity risks, a Forescout study finds

item.title: Introducing the Agent Readiness score. Is your site agent-ready?
item.description: The Agent Readiness score can help site owners understand how well their websites support AI agents. Here we explore new standards, share Radar data, and detail how we made Cloudflare’s docs the most agent-friendly on...

item.title: Redirects for AI Training enforces canonical content
item.description: Soft directives don’t stop crawlers from ingesting deprecated content. Redirects for AI Training allows anybody on Cloudflare to redirect verified crawlers to canonical pages with one toggle and no origin changes.

item.title: Unweight: how we compressed an LLM 22% without sacrificing quality
item.description: Running LLMs across Cloudflare’s network requires us to be smarter and more efficient about GPU memory bandwidth. That’s why we developed Unweight, a lossless inference-time compression system that achieves up to a 22...

item.title: Agents that remember: introducing Agent Memory
item.description: Cloudflare Agent Memory is a managed service that gives AI agents persistent memory, allowing them to recall what matters, forget what doesn't, and get smarter over time.

item.title: Agents Week: network performance update
item.description: By migrating our request handling layer to a Rust-based architecture called FL2, Cloudflare has increased its performance lead to 60% of the world’s top networks. We use real-user measurements and connection trimeans...

item.title: Introducing Flagship: feature flags built for the age of AI
item.description: We are launching Flagship, a native feature flag service built on Cloudflare’s global network to eliminate the latency of third-party providers. By using KV and Durable Objects, Flagship allows for sub-millisecond fla...

item.title: Researcher drops two more Microsoft Defender zero-days, all three now exploited in the wild
item.description: The security researcher who earlier this month published a proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit for a zero-day privilege escalation vulnerability in Microsoft Defender is back with two more. The first, dubbed “RedSun,” is a...

item.title: Recent Apache ActiveMQ Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild
item.description: The remote code execution vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-34197 came to light in early April. The post Recent Apache ActiveMQ Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: GitLab 18.11 brings agentic AI to security fixes, CI pipelines, and delivery analytics
item.description: GitLab has released GitLab 18.11, expanding agentic AI across the entire software lifecycle with security remediation, pipeline configuration, and delivery analytics. AI-generated code moves faster than the systems ar...

item.title: CISA flags Apache ActiveMQ flaw as actively exploited in attacks
item.description: CISA warned that attackers are now exploiting a high-severity Apache ActiveMQ vulnerability, which was patched earlier this month after going undetected for 13 years. [...]

item.title: Liongard upgrades LiongardIQ with AI access, live asset data, and deeper discovery
item.description: Liongard has announced the expansion of LiongardIQ with new capabilities spanning programmatic AI integration, conversational querying, enhanced network discovery, and deeper identity mapping, extending its system of...

item.title: Two North Korean IT Worker Scheme Facilitators Jailed in the US
item.description: Kejia Wang and Zhenxing Wang compromised the identities of dozens of US persons to help land jobs at over 100 companies. The post Two North Korean IT Worker Scheme Facilitators Jailed in the US appeared first on Secur...

item.title: Inside ZionSiphon: politically driven malware aims at Israeli water systems
item.description: New ZionSiphon malware targets water systems, and allows attackers to alter pressure and chlorine levels. A flaw makes it ineffective for now. Darktrace analyzed ZionSiphon, a new malware designed to target water trea...

item.title: Mozilla challenges enterprise AI providers with Thunderbolt, open-source AI client under your control
item.description: For organizations that want to keep company data within their own systems and have more control over how AI is deployed, Mozilla is offering an alternative to externally hosted AI services with Thunderbolt, an open-so...

item.title: Codex can now operate between apps. Where are the boundaries?
item.description: OpenAI is rolling out a major update to the Codex desktop app for users signed in with ChatGPT. Personalization features, including context-aware suggestions and memory, will roll out to Enterprise, Edu, and users in...

item.title: ZionSiphon Malware Targets ICS in Water Facilities
item.description: The malware is configured to operate on systems associated with Israeli water treatment and desalination plants. The post ZionSiphon Malware Targets ICS in Water Facilities appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Microsoft: Some Windows servers enter reboot loops after April patches
item.description: Microsoft warns that some Windows domain controllers are entering restart loops after installing the April 2026 security updates. [...]

item.title: Cursor AI Vulnerability Exposed Developer Devices
item.description: An indirect prompt injection could be chained with a sandbox bypass and Cursor’s remote tunnel feature for shell access to machines. The post Cursor AI Vulnerability Exposed Developer Devices appeared first on Securit...

item.title: NIST Limits CVE Enrichment After 263% Surge in Vulnerability Submissions
item.description: The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) has announced changes to the way it handles cybersecurity vulnerabilities and exposures (CVEs) listed in its National Vulnerability Database (NVD), stating it...

item.title: Android 17 Beta 4 arrives with post-quantum cryptography and new memory limits
item.description: Google shipped Android 17 Beta 4 on April 16, marking the last scheduled beta in the Android 17 release cycle. The build targets app compatibility testing and platform stability ahead of the final release, and it carr...

item.title: Apple AirTag tracking can be misled by replayed Bluetooth signals
item.description: Apple’s AirTag is designed to help users track lost items by relying on a vast network of nearby Apple devices. New research shows that this same system can be manipulated to display locations where an AirTag has neve...

item.title: Social media bans might steer kids into riskier corners of the internet
item.description: Governments are moving to block children under 16 from social media in the name of safety. But once these measures move from policy to practice, they raise a harder question: what happens when protecting kids requires...

item.title: Apache ActiveMQ CVE-2026-34197 Added to CISA KEV Amid Active Exploitation
item.description: A recently disclosed high-severity security flaw in Apache ActiveMQ Classic has come under active exploitation in the wild, per the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA). To that end, the agency...

item.title: From air-gapped to private cloud: Security that adapts to your environment
item.description: Organizations operate across SaaS, private cloud, on-premises, and air-gapped environments, each shaped by specific regulatory, operational, and connectivity constraints. Sysdig Secure supports these deployment models...

item.title: North Korea Uses ClickFix to Target macOS Users' Data
item.description: Sapphire Sleet uses fake job offers and phony Zoom updates to deliver ClickFix attacks that steal credentials and sensitive data from Macs.

item.title: US Nationals Jailed for Operating Fake Remote Worker Laptop Farms for North Korea
item.description: US authorities jail two Americans for aiding North Korean laptop farm scams that infiltrated over 100 firms

item.title: APK Malformation Found in Thousands of Android Malware Samples
item.description: APK malformation tactic now appears in over 3000 Android malware samples evading static analysis

item.title: Two-Factor Authentication Breaks Free from the Desktop
item.description: Threat actors know how to bypass security systems outside of traditional IT environments. Implementing 2FA could provide a needed extra security barrier in the physical world.

item.title: Microsoft's Original Windows Secure Boot Certificate Is Expiring
item.description: The Secure Boot refresh is one of the largest coordinated security maintenance efforts across the Windows ecosystem, Microsoft said. Update those PCs soon.

item.title: Cookeville Medical Center Notifies Patients After July 2025 Ransomware Attack
item.description: Tennessee's CRMC notifies over 337,000 patients of Rhysida ransomware breach exposing sensitive data

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Defender 0-Day, SonicWall Brute-Force, 17-Year-Old Excel RCE and 15 More Stories
item.description: You know that feeling when you open your feed on a Thursday morning and it's just... a lot? Yeah. This week delivered. We've got hackers getting creative in ways that are almost impressive if you ignore the whole "cri...
